learnings: Nginx SSL requirement in local dev can crash the proxy; 401 Unauthorized handling is critical for authenticated dashboards.

insights: Pattern: Hard guardrails for visual consistency prevent design drift during targeted page updates
Pattern: Anchor Pages (Search, Chat, Enrichment) serve as the visual DNA source of truth for the OODA loop
Decision: Shell Layout (Sidebar, Background) mismatches are now automatic FAIL blockers
Protocol: Human-in-the-Loop promotion protocol allows page-specific upgrades to be applied globally
Tooling: Dev/Audit bypass in Playwright allows verification without manual credentials
next_steps: Test the OODA loop upgrade protocol during the next Dashboard remediation
Verify that the Auth Bypass correctly handles session-protected pages
Refine the Sidebar drift threshold (currently 80%) based on real-world usage

insights: Pattern: Permission mode 'acceptEdits' allows for faster autonomous memory synthesis and deployment
Decision: Synchronized all skills and agents across Claude, Gemini, and Codex to ensure environment parity
Pattern: Hard guardrails for visual consistency are now the default for the OODA loop
next_steps: Test the autonomous OODA loop on the next dashboard remediation
Document the cross-platform sync protocol in the project wiki
